title: Ratio-Dependent Contrarian Activation in Opinion Dynamics
abstract: I study the impact of mixed contrarians on the opinion dynamics of an heterogenous population with conformists using Galam Majority Model. Activation of contrarians is a function of the ratio majority/minority in the local groups of discussion. Restricting the group size to 3, two types of contrarians are included in respective proportions $c_{3,0}$ for configurations with ratio 3 to 0 and $c_{2,1}$ for ratio 2 to 1. I then derive the explicit update Equation and obtained analytically the fixed points, their stability, and the resulting full two-dimensional landscape of the dynamics of opinion. Setting $c_{3,0} =c_{2,1} = c$ recovers the original results obtained with uniform contrarians. The findings allow for considering a wide spectrum of new disruptive strategies to secure either a majority/minority ending ensuring the opinion having the larger initial support to win, or a single attractor dynamics at fifty/fifty, which implies a random winner regardless of initial supports.
summary: This paper extends the Galam Majority Model by making contrarian behavior dependent on the local ratio of opinions in a group. It reveals a complex landscape of dynamics, showing how specific contrarian strategies can either reinforce a majority or drive a system toward a 50/50 polarized state.
main_problem: Investigating how opinion dynamics change when the activation of contrarians depends on the local majority/minority ratio rather than being a uniform probability.
main_result: The study identifies a two-dimensional landscape of four distinct dynamical domains, including regimes of majority/minority consensus, polarization (50/50 attractor), and alternating dynamics.
method: Analytical derivation of update equations for the Galam Majority Model, followed by fixed point and stability analysis using derivatives and Taylor expansion.
model_or_system: An extension of the Galam Majority Model (GMM) involving discussion groups of size 3 with agents acting as conformists or ratio-dependent contrarians.
key_observables: Proportion of agents with opinion A (p_n), fixed points (p_c), and stability of attractors.
important_parameters: c_3,0 (contrarian probability in unanimous groups), c_2,1 (contrarian probability in 2:1 groups), and the uniform limit c.
main_assumptions: The model is restricted to group sizes of 3 and assumes the validity of the dynamics for contrarian probabilities between 0 and 1.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a phase diagram of the dynamical domains; Figure 5 illustrates the variation of opinion levels as a function of c_2,1 for various fixed c_3,0.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of mixed contrarians, defines the extended Galam Majority Model, provides the analytical derivation of update equations, performs fixed point and stability analysis to map the dynamical landscape, and discusses strategic implications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The two domains of the landscape of the dynamics induced by the variation of c3,0 and c2,1. The domains are separated by the line c3,0 = 1 3(1 −3c2,1). Inside the colored area, pA,B exist and are attractors with pc being a tipping point. Outside the colored area in the white area only the fixed point pc = 1
Figure 2: Various main stages of the variation pA in red and pB in blue as a function of c2,1 for the series of given values c3,0 = 0, 0.01, 0.17, 0.3, 0.35, 0.99. The fixed point pc = 1
Figure 3: The complete landscape of the dynamics including alternating regimes. Four domains are shown as a function of c3,0 and c2,1. The domains are separated by the lines c3,0 = 1

Figure 4: Four different regimes produced by the update Equation (11) with a tipping point coupled to two attractors, a single attractor, an alternating single attractor and an alternating tipping point coupled to two alternating attractors, for respectively (c3,0 = 0.05, c2,1 = 0.15), (c3,0 = 0.12, c2,1 = 0.22), (c3,0 = 0.88, c2,1 = 0.78), (c3,0 = 0.95, c2,1 = 0.85).
Figure 5: Various main stages of the variation p ¯ A in red and p ¯B in blue as a function of c2,1 for the series of given values c3,0 = 0, 0.01, 0.17, 0.3, 0.35, 0.99. The fixed point pc = 1
